summary: Generate images, videos, and text/LLM completions via the imgnAI Katana API. Supports end-to-end-encrypted (E2EE) and anonymized models. Priced highly compet...

latestVersion.changelog:
Added
  •  q-naifu-a3b  text model — imgnAI fully uncensored Agentic Model (Private tier, 262K context, vision + file input)
  • Audio output docs for video models
  • Three-tier privacy documentation — Anonymized, Private, E2EE Private with attestation details
  • Non-OpenClaw setup guide in README

Changed
  • Claude alias now points to  claude-opus-4-8 ; added  claude-fast  for  claude-opus-4-8-fast
  • Poll timeout guard: 10 min for image/text, 100 min for video (matches API limits)
  • Persistence file supports  KATANA_STATE_DIR  to separate state from credentials

Fixed
  • Text submission auth: curl commands now use single  && -chained line (fixes failures on some agents)
  • Payload temp files are now cleaned up after each request
  • Header auth bug: submit and poll now use consistent two-header format
  •  gpt-image-2-max  reference image count: 12 → 10
  •  ***  literal removed from header format strings

displayName: Reptile Feeding Refusal / Vomiting Detection | 爬宠进食拒绝/呕吐识别
summary: Through fixed enclosure cameras, the system analyzes feeding-time and post-feeding videos of reptiles (snakes, lizards, turtles) to detect prey-attack behavi...

latestVersion.changelog:
smyx-reptile-feeding-refusal-vomiting-analysis 1.0.0 – Initial release

- Detects and analyzes reptile feeding and post-feeding behaviors (attack, swallowing, regurgitation) via enclosure cameras.
- Identifies feeding refusal and vomiting events, providing structured alerts and recommendations based on species and physiological context.
- Implements strict open-id authentication for event recording and cloud-based history queries.
- Supports integration with smart vivariums and reptile-keeping apps for automatic monitoring and notification.
- Enforces privacy, event-reporting, and intervention boundaries; avoids overreaching into diagnosis or forced care actions.
- Provides bilingual (English/Chinese) documentation for ease of use in varied application scenarios.

displayName: Spillover: Animal Infections and the Next Human Pandemic

latestVersion.changelog:
Initial release of the Spillover skill — a guide to zoonotic diseases and pandemic origins based on David Quammen’s book.

- Covers key events and outbreaks: Hendra, Nipah, SARS, Ebola, HIV/AIDS.
- Includes 7 guided use cases such as "What is Zoonosis?", "How did HIV start?", and "Can we predict the next pandemic?".
- Auto-displays a quick start guide on first use, including sample questions.
- Explains ecological drivers, animal reservoirs (especially bats), and methods of prevention.
- Responds to a wide range of relevant trigger words and phrases related to pandemics and zoonotic disease.

summary: Generate React design drafts (4-piece set) from content. Invoke for 'design draft'/'设计稿'/'生成页面'/'信息图'/'知识卡片'/'多图配图'. Do NOT use for editing existing code.

latestVersion.changelog:
- Major release: Adds automatic, multi-illustration, and brand-adaptive design modes for generating React design drafts.
- New "Master Mode" for fully automated, confirmation-free generation with style auto-matching and batch previews.
- Introduces Multi-Illustration Mode (with 5-step flow, including article parsing and per-illustration edit guides).
- Enhanced brand profiling and "Brand DNA" auto-detection; now supports explicit brand style configuration.
- Expanded content readiness and quality filters; stronger checks for visualizability, density, and anti-generic writing.
- Adds references for brand profile, chart-system, and multi-illustrations; updates all guides and mappings for new workflows.

Initial release of skill-creator, a comprehensive guide for designing modular skills to extend Claude’s capabilities.

- Introduces principles for creating effective, concise, and domain-specific skills.
- Explains modular structure: required SKILL.md and optional scripts, references, and assets folders.
- Outlines guidelines for what to include (and exclude) in a skill package.
- Details progressive disclosure design for efficient context management.
- Provides reference patterns for organizing content and bundling resources.
